diagrams.title: Array Memory Layout
diagrams.description: Shows how elements are stored contiguously in memory with indices
diagrams.purpose: Understanding direct access and contiguous storage

diagrams.title: Structure vs Union Memory Diagram
diagrams.description: Compares memory allocation in structure (separate) vs union (shared)
elements: Structure: Member A at offset 0, Member B at offset 4, Member C at offset 8
Union: All members at offset 0, total size = largest member

diagrams.title: Row-Major vs Column-Major 2D Array Storage
diagrams.description: Shows how 2D array elements are mapped to 1D memory
elements: Array[2][3]: Row-Major stores as row0col0, row0col1, row0col2, row1col0, row1col1, row1col2. Column-Major stores as col0row0, col1row0, col2row0, col0row1, col1row1, col2row1

diagrams.title: Sparse Matrix Triplet Representation
diagrams.description: Shows how a sparse matrix is stored using only non-zero elements
